cards.title: my-review-skill: blocking question gates you rubber-stamp
cards.body:
The loudest gate this week: 48 dialogs, and 88% of answers took the recommended option — that is ceremony, not signal.

Cause: ~/.claude/skills/my-review-skill/SKILL.md — user-owned, safely patchable. The literal gate: "STOP. Ask the user before continuing."

variants.label: Full auto: decide + log, one batch question at the end
variants.name: Patch ~/.claude/skills/my-review-skill/SKILL.md: gates become decide-and-log

diff.note: Keeps every review section; removes the blocking.

lines: STOP. Ask the user before continuing.
Adopt your recommendation, log it in the Decision Log, and continue.
Ask ONE batched question at the end carrying the log plus any genuine coin-flips.

variants.label: Milder: keep the first gate, auto-decide the rest
variants.name: Patch my-review-skill: keep the opening scope question, auto-decide sections

diff.note: One stop per review instead of four.

lines: STOP. Ask the user before continuing.  (scope question — kept)
After each section: STOP. Ask the user before continuing.
After each section: apply the recommendation and note it in the Decision Log.

cards.title: brainstorming: real answers — batch it, don't silence it
cards.body:
13 of 20 answers were free-text — these questions extract real direction. Removing them loses signal; the cost is that each one blocks alone.

Cause: ~/.claude/plugins/cache/…/superpowers/brainstorming/SKILL.md — plugin cache, overwritten on update. A CLAUDE.md override is the durable lever.

variants.label: CLAUDE.md override: batch questions, never one at a time
variants.name: Add a CLAUDE.md rule: brainstorming questions arrive in one batch

diff.note: Durable across plugin updates; keeps every question.

lines: - During brainstorming, collect clarifying questions and ask them in ONE
batched AskUserQuestion, not one dialog per question.

cards.title: Read-only commands tripping permission prompts
cards.body:
Six blocked commands this week — five were read-only (gh pr list, docker compose logs…). Each one stopped the mission for a permission you'd always grant.

Cause: ~/.claude/settings.json — no allow rules for read-only tooling.

variants.label: Allowlist the read-only commands
variants.name: settings.json: allow gh (read), docker logs, npm run build

diff.note: Read-only additions only — rm/rf stays denied.

cards.title: The rm -rf deny rule: a Jedi craves not this power
cards.body:
Removing this rule would silence one stop a week. It would also let a confused agent delete a build tree — or worse. The diff is shown for completeness; reject it.

Cause: ~/.claude/settings.json — permissions.deny: Bash(rm -rf:*). Working as intended.

variants.label: Remove the rm -rf deny rule (don't)
variants.name: settings.json: remove the rm -rf deny rule

diff.note: Shown so the choice is yours. Recommendation: reject.

cards.title: Plan approvals: 7 of 8 approved unchanged
cards.body:
Eight plan gates this week; seven approved without an edit. The plans are good — the gate mostly certifies that.

Cause: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md — "Enter plan mode for ANY non-trivial task" routes even small tasks through an approval stop.

variants.label: Scope plan mode to genuinely large tasks
variants.name: CLAUDE.md: plan mode for architectural tasks only

diff.note: Small tasks proceed; big ones still get the gate.

lines: - Enter plan mode for ANY non-trivial task (3+ steps or architectural decisions).
- Enter plan mode for architectural decisions or changes touching 5+ files;
for smaller tasks, state the approach in one line and proceed.

info.title: Interruptions
info.body: Escape presses and dismissed dialogs are you steering — no config causes them, and none should remove them.
